qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Hardcoded production OAuth credentials enable credential theft and impersonation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies hardcoded OAuth credentials in src/auth/auth.ts:269-270 of the correct clasp repository, but the root cause is credential disclosure (hardcoded secrets, CWE-798), not path traversal. The advisory describes missing path sanitization in the clone/pull commands that allows writing files outside the project directory (CWE-22). These are fundamentally different vulnerability classes with different vulnerable code, different attack surfaces, and different security consequences.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes a path traversal vulnerability (CWE-22) in @google/clasp where the clone and pull commands fail to sanitize file paths from a malicious remote script, allowing files to be written outside the intended project directory. The only qualifying finding ('hardcoded-oauth-credentials') correctly targets the clasp repository but addresses hardcoded OAuth credentials (CWE-798) in src/auth/auth.ts:269-270, which is an entirely different root cause and vulnerability class from the path traversal in clone/pull file-writing logic.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-hqjg-pww4-pcgq describes path traversal (CWE-22) in clasp clone/pull commands, fixed in v3.2.0 via commit ba6bd666.

Finding 'hardcoded-oauth-credentials' references src/auth/auth.ts:269-270 which is in the clasp codebase, but the vulnerability is hardcoded OAuth credentials (CWE-798), not path traversal (CWE-22). The root cause, attack surface, and security consequence are all different from the advisory.
